Bus Timings

Abu Dhabi 222 Bus Timings from Osama Bin Zaid Mosque to Hili Street

October 13, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

The Abu Dhabi public transportation system offers an efficient and affordable way to travel throughout the city, and Bus 222 is no exception. This route connects Osama Bin Zaid Mosque to Hili Street, Souq, providing access to various residential areas, markets, and community services along the way.

Operational Details

Bus 222 operates regularly, ensuring that passengers have access to consistent service throughout the day. Below are the key timings for Bus 222 departing from both Osama Bin Zaid Mosque and Hili Street, Souq:

From Osama Bin Zaid Mosque From Hili Street
05:33 05:43
07:31 07:42
09:30 09:39
11:30 11:41
13:31 13:43
15:30 15:41
17:30 17:41
19:30 19:43
21:30 21:46
23:31 23:46

Bus 222 Timetable

For the complete timetable, you can check the official website of AD Mobility or visit this link for the Bus 222 Timings.

Ticket Pricing: Passengers can conveniently use the Hafilat Card to pay for their Bus 222 trips. The fare structure is straightforward and affordable, with a fee of AED 2 for travel within one zone and an additional AED 0.05 per kilometer for longer journeys. Hafilat Cards can be topped up easily online or at kiosks across Abu Dhabi, providing a seamless and cashless travel experience.

Route Overview

Bus 222 operates between Osama Bin Zaid Mosque and Hili Street, Souq, passing through numerous key locations that showcase the local community. Below is a comprehensive list of important stops along this route:

  1. Osama Bin Zaid Mosque A prominent local mosque marking the journey’s start.

  2. Al Dameed Street A residential area with essential amenities.

  3. Hassan Bin Thabit Mosque A notable spiritual landmark along the way.

  4. Bad‘ Bu ‘Ireesh Street A quieter street primarily for local traffic.

5.

  1. Al ‘Uqalaa Street A residential area providing access to local homes.

  2. Ghabat Milaysah Street Another residential street in the vicinity.

  3. New Shahama Park A recreational area offering green space for families.

  4. Barqa Bu Hasa Street A quiet neighborhood street.

  5. Al Aslah Street A local street providing community access.

  6. Green Mart Market A convenient grocery store for daily needs.

  7. Al Shahama Bus Station A central transit hub for further connections.

  8. Halima Al Sa’adeya School Catering to the educational needs of the community.

  9. Shuhadah Badar Mosque A local mosque serving the area.

  10. Emirates Transport A key stop for transportation services.

  11. Al Wahda Sport Club Academy A popular location for sports training.

  12. Federal Government Office A stop for administrative services.

  13. Street 15 A local street with residential access.

  14. Al Tarawwi Street Another residential area along the route.

  15. Al Tari Street Providing access to local homes.

  16. Abu Al Darda Mosque A spiritual stop for residents.

  17. Al Rahba Hospital A crucial healthcare facility along the route.

  18. Al Rahba Plaza A local shopping and community area.

  19. Street 4 A residential street providing access to homes.

  20. Al Ahnaf Bin Qais Mosque Another important spiritual site.

  21. Al Nawabigh Street A street connecting various neighborhoods.

  22. Al Rahba School Catering to the educational needs of local children.

  23. Street 54 A residential area providing local access.

  24. Sheikh Hamad bin Mohammed Mosque A significant spiritual landmark.

  25. Street 76 Another residential street along the route.

  26. Al Rahba Park A public park for relaxation and recreation.

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Al Rahba Souq A traditional market offering various goods.

  2. Street 12 A neighborhood street with residential access.

  3. Street 42 Another local street serving the community.

  4. Street 70 A street connecting various residential areas.

  5. Al Yal Street A local road providing access to homes.

  6. Muhammad Thani Matar Al Rumaithi Mosque A significant local mosque.

  7. Park A community green space.

  8. Uhud Martyrs Mosque A spiritual site honoring local heroes.

  9. Abu Mousa Al Shaiery Mosque Another important mosque along the route.

  10. ADNOC A convenient stop for fuel and services.

  11. Street 17 A local street connecting neighborhoods.

  12. Al ‘Ama-ir Street A road serving the residential community.

  13. Healthcare Center A facility providing essential health services.

  14. Sheikh Hamdan bin Zayed Al Nahyan Mosque A notable mosque in the area.

  15. Raja’a Bin Haywa Mosque Another local spiritual landmark.

  16. Al Reef 2 A residential area with several stops.

  17. Al Samha Street A street connecting various neighborhoods.

  18. Mawasem Park A recreational park for families.

  19. Street 82 A local road serving the community.

  20. Mattar Al Rumaithi Mosque A significant mosque in the area.

  21. Al ‘Amirah Street A street providing access to homes.

53.

Accessibility and Services

Bus 222 is designed to accommodate passengers with special needs, ensuring that everyone can enjoy a comfortable journey. The buses are equipped with:

  • Ramps for wheelchair access.

  • Designated seating for those with mobility issues.
  • Audio-visual aids for passengers with hearing or vision impairments. This inclusive approach makes Bus 222 a suitable option for all commuters.

Nearby Attractions and Landmarks

Traveling on Bus 222 provides access to several attractions and landmarks in Abu Dhabi:

  1. Hili Archaeological Park Nearby, this historic site showcases ancient artifacts and remnants, making it a great spot for history enthusiasts.

2.

  1. Hili Fun City A popular amusement park, perfect for families seeking entertainment and fun.

Journey Experience

On a recent trip along Bus 222 from Osama Bin Zaid Mosque to Hili Street, Souq, travelers experienced a scenic journey through Abu Dhabi.

Notable stops included New Shahama Park, a lovely green space for families, and Al Rahba Hospital, an essential healthcare facility. Arriving at Hili Street, Souq, passengers enjoyed a lively shopping scene with traditional goods and eateries, making the trip a delightful blend of convenience and cultural exploration.

Travel Tips for Bus 222

  • Plan Ahead: Check the bus timings to avoid unnecessary waiting, especially during peak hours.

  • Use the Hafilat Smart Card: This enables quick, cashless payments on all Abu Dhabi buses.
  • Monitor Bus Updates: Use the AD Mobility app for real-time updates and schedules to optimize your journey.
  • Enjoy the Stops: Take advantage of the varied stops along the route to explore local attractions and markets.

Bus 222 provides a dependable and convenient route between Osama Bin Zaid Mosque and Hili Street, Souq.

Whether you’re heading to work, shopping at the Souq, or relaxing in local parks, Bus 222 guarantees a smooth and affordable travel experience through Abu Dhabi’s vibrant neighborhoods. Jump on Bus 222 and explore the unique charm of the city! Be sure to explore the full range of bus routes available in Abu Dhabi for more travel options!

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)
